what_it_does: This file is a project wayfinder / registry index for the counterpart-design repository. It maps conceptual concerns to file addresses, lists a spine of key files with reference counts, types, and summaries, and gives per-folder address totals, acting as a navigational ledger of code/design substrates.
observations: meta reports 669 addresses but only a small subset appear in by_concern and spine
most by_concern entries have empty addrs arrays
spine entries all have refs:0 and blank summaries
by_location shows review (266) and pieces (85) as the largest folders
profiled, edges, and ghosts metrics are null or zero everywhere
labels use a unicode arrow (rule → number)
standouts: The by_concern taxonomy names eleven conceptual territories from identity tokens to the registry itself
669 total addresses are declared while detailed profiling fields remain empty
The spine lists key HTML/JSON/MDN files but carries no summaries or references
The review folder contains 266 files, far more than any other folder
concerns: Spine entries carry no summaries or reference counts despite the schema providing those fields
Many concern categories have empty address lists, weakening the map
meta.profiled, edges, and ghosts are null, indicating the registry has not been fully populated
There is a large gap between the declared 669 addresses and the addresses explicitly listed
notes: A high-level map that names the conceptual territories of the project but has not yet been populated with detailed per-file profiles or cross-references.
questions: Which 669 addresses are counted and where are they enumerated?
Why are refs and summaries zero/blank across the entire spine?
What distinguishes the profiled, edges, and ghosts metrics in this ledger?
Are empty concern address lists placeholders or genuinely unassigned categories?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is a navigational registry/wayfinder; actual content provides a coarse concern map and folder census but lacks the detailed cross-references and profiles the schema implies, so it is only partially realized.
intention_signals: To create a semantic index over a large design codebase so humans and tools can locate substrates by concept and by location, and to track profiling coverage over time.
novelty: Treats the codebase as a ledger of code/design substrates with a concern-based ontology and a self-referential 'registry (this system)' category.
contribution: Provides a stable, human-readable concern taxonomy and a spine/location census that a fused system can use as a navigational scaffold and coverage checklist, independent of detailed symbol analysis.
summary_for_embedding: registry/wayfinder.json is a coarse-grained project wayfinder that categorizes counterpart-design substrates by concern, lists a spine of key files, and reports per-folder address totals with mostly unpopulated profiling fields.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to serve as a semantic map and coverage ledger for navigating the project's design-code substrates.
